meta.title: Magical Harp with Distance Sensor
meta.description: Create a harp that plays a tone when an object is detected within range using an ultrasonic sensor and buzzer.
meta.learning_goal: Integrating sensor input with actuator output for interactive projects
meta.code_hint: Use HC_SR04 to measure distance, then trigger BUZZER tone when object is within threshold

meta.title: Magical Harp with Distance Sensor
meta.description: Create a harp that plays a tone when an object is detected within range using an ultrasonic sensor and buzzer.
meta.learning_goal: Integrating sensor input with actuator output for interactive projects
meta.code_hint: Use HC_SR04 to measure distance, then trigger BUZZER tone when object is within threshold

walkthrough.instruction: Plug the HC-SR04 sensor into the breadboard with all four pins in column J: VCC in row 12, TRIG in row 11, ECHO in row 10, GND in row 9.
walkthrough.tip: The two silver circles are the ultrasonic 'eyes' — point them toward your target!

walkthrough.instruction: Place the buzzer with the long leg (+) in row 13, column E and the short leg (−) in row 16, column E.
walkthrough.tip: The long leg is positive — mark it with a tiny + on the buzzer.

walkthrough.instruction: Connect a red wire from the Arduino's 5V pin to the breadboard at row 12, column I.
walkthrough.tip: This wire carries the signal between Arduino and your component.

walkthrough.instruction: Connect a black wire from the Arduino's GND pin to the breadboard at row 9, column I.
walkthrough.tip: Black wires carry ground — the return path for electricity.

walkthrough.instruction: Connect a green wire from Arduino pin D9 to the breadboard at row 11, column F.
walkthrough.tip: This wire carries the signal between Arduino and your component.

walkthrough.instruction: Connect a green wire from the breadboard at row 10, column F to Arduino pin D10.
walkthrough.tip: This wire carries the signal between Arduino and your component.

walkthrough.instruction: Connect a green wire from Arduino pin D3 to the breadboard at row 13, column A.
walkthrough.tip: This wire carries the signal between Arduino and your component.

walkthrough.instruction: Connect a black wire from the breadboard at row 16, column D to the Arduino's GND pin.
walkthrough.tip: Black wires carry ground — the return path for electricity.
